mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and industrial production indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and stock prices higher over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window or 60-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles characterized by rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals improved manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window or 20-day returns in high-industrial production regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Weak payroll growth indicates slack in the labor market that constrains consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales finance indicate robust consumer demand, driving equity prices higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window or 20-day returns in high-demand regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing market booms reflected in rising housing starts signal increased consumer confidence and wealth effects, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal.
